mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting aggregate income and consumption demand, causing equity prices to fall over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, month-over-month declines in PAYEMS are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising by more than 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window triggers labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls generate a joint demand contraction that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS changes adds no incremental explanatory power (t-stat < 2) in a rolling 3-year regression for 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity values and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During tightening cycles, month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining industrial production index signals contracting manufacturing output and corporate revenue compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 2.0 percent, aggressive monetary easing lowers borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Instances with FEDFUNDS below 2.0% are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts combined with falling retail sales indicate a synchronized deterioration in consumer spending and residential investment, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month declines in HOUST and RSAFS are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of joint-decline episodes over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate increases household financial stress and reduces aggregate consumption, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month rises in UNRATE are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences during any 3-year sample.
